items.why: Students reverse IV and DV. The IV is what you CHANGE (input); the DV is what you MEASURE (outcome). Mnemonic: 'I change the I.'

items.q: Which part of the psyche operates on the reality principle?
items.why: Students confuse ego (reality) with superego (morality). The ego mediates between id's demands and reality's constraints. The superego is the internalised moral conscience.

items.correct: Learning by observing the consequences others receive for behaviour.
items.misconception: Being directly rewarded for copying someone.
items.why: Students confuse vicarious with direct reinforcement. 'Vicarious' means experienced through someone else - you see them rewarded/punished and adjust your own behaviour accordingly.

items.q: Bowlby's monotropy claim says babies form:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: One main attachment. “No attachments” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Behaviours like smiling that trigger caregiving are called:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Social releasers. “Reflexes” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Bowlby's critical period is said to last about how many years?
items.why: The real figure is 2.5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
